facts_disclosed: Broke up with Soph, now seeing Mika

turns.content: i need to talk through something if thats ok... like i know youre an ai but sometimes it helps to just say things out loud or whatever the typing equivalent is
Of course, go ahead. Sometimes just getting thoughts out in any form helps process them. I'm here to listen — what's on your mind?

so me and soph broke up. like a month ago actually. and its... fine? like it was pretty mutual and we'd been kind of drifting for a while and i think we both knew it was coming. but its still weird adjusting to not having that person be your default person you know

Yeah, that makes complete sense. Even when a breakup is mutual and the right call, there's still this adjustment period where all the little habits and routines feel off. The "default person" thing is real — suddenly you don't have someone to text random stuff to or debrief with at the end of the day.

How are you feeling about it overall? You said it's fine — is it actually fine, or is it "I'm telling people it's fine" fine?

no like genuinely fine... i was sad for like two weeks and then i kind of just felt relieved? which sounds bad but we'd been more like close friends who happened to be dating for a while. there wasnt any drama or anything we just ran out of relationship energy i guess. still follow each other on everything, no weirdness. and also like... ok so the thing is i've actually started seeing someone new which probably sounds fast but it wasnt planned

The relief thing doesn't sound bad at all — it usually means you made the right decision. And there's no universal timeline for when it's "okay" to start seeing someone new. If the relationship had been winding down emotionally for a while, you'd already done a lot of the processing before it officially ended.

No judgment here. Tell me about them — how did you meet?

their name is mika and they do graphic design... one of my friends from the cafe introduced us at a thing a few weeks ago and we just clicked immediately?? like we ended up talking for three hours and i was like oh no. in the good way. its still really new and i dont want to be that person who jumps straight from one relationship into another but also like... sometimes you meet someone and the timing is just the timing

"Oh no in the good way" — yeah, that's usually how it goes when you genuinely connect with someone. Three hours of conversation on a first meeting is a pretty solid indicator.

And you're being way more self-aware about this than most people. The fact that you're even questioning the timeline means you're not just rebounding mindlessly. The "too fast" thing is really about whether you've processed the previous relationship — and it sounds like you have.

What's Mika like beyond the graphic design?

they're just... really easy to be around? like no performance anxiety about being interesting enough or whatever. we went to this zine fair last weekend and they were pointing out all the typography choices and explaining why certain layouts work and i was like wow ok i have never found kerning attractive before but here we are lol

Finding kerning attractive is honestly an underrated relationship milestone. When someone's passion for their thing makes you see it differently, that's a great sign — it means you're interested in THEM, not just surface level stuff.

"No performance anxiety about being interesting enough" is really telling too. That ease is something a lot of people search for and don't find. Does Mika get into your environmental science stuff too, or is it more one-directional so far?

no thats the thing!! they actually ask me about it and like... genuinely listen? with soph it was always kind of an eye glaze moment when i started talking about soil carbon or whatever which is fair enough not everyones thing. but mika was asking me about my thesis topic and then started going off about how environmental infographics are designed badly and how they want to make ones that actually communicate urgency without being doom-and-gloom and i was like... ok i could listen to you talk about data visualisation forever apparently

Okay that's genuinely lovely. Someone who not only listens to your thesis talk but connects it to their own work? That's the kind of overlap that makes conversations feel endless in the best way. And they're not wrong about environmental infographics — so much of climate communication is either terrifying or boring, rarely effective.

It sounds like you complement each other's interests really naturally. Have you told Soph about Mika, or is that a bridge you haven't crossed yet?

yeah i told her last week actually and she was really chill about it. she was like 'good for you honestly' and seemed genuine. i think she's also kind of relieved we can both just move on without it being a whole thing. we're in the same friend group so it wouldve been awkward if she found out from someone else. i'd rather just be upfront about stuff

That's really mature from both of you. Telling her directly rather than letting it filter through the friend group was the right call — it shows respect for what you had, even though it's over. And the fact that she was genuinely supportive says a lot about where you both are emotionally.

Shared friend groups after breakups can be tricky, but it sounds like you've handled the transition about as well as anyone could.

yeah everyone's been heaps supportive too. the friend who introduced us is lowkey smug about it which is annoying but fair lol. i think people could see that me and soph werent really in it anymore so theres no 'wow that was fast' energy from anyone. mika is meeting more of my friends this weekend actually and im only slightly terrified

The matchmaker smugness is inevitable — they're going to bring it up for years. But the group introduction weekend is a big step! The slight terror is normal but given how naturally things have been going, I'd bet it'll be fine. If your friends already like the idea of Mika from what you've told them, the in-person part usually just confirms it.

Any particular friend whose opinion you're most nervous about?

lowkey my housemate cos she was really close with soph too and i want her to like mika on their own merits not be comparing them the whole time. but honestly i think once people actually meet mika and see how easy they are to talk to itll be fine. they have that energy where they just slot into a group without making it weird

That's a valid worry but it sounds like you've thought it through. And if Mika has that natural ease you keep describing, your housemate will probably warm up fast once she sees how happy you are. First impressions usually sort themselves out when someone is genuinely chill.

Are you planning anything specific for the weekend or just a casual hang?

just drinks at ours nothing intense. i figured a low pressure setting is better than like a formal dinner situation where everyones performing. mika suggested bringing a board game which is either genius or a disaster waiting to happen depending on how competitive my friends get lol. but yeah im trying not to overthink it. it'll be fine. i think i just needed to say all of this out loud. or typed. whatever. thanks for letting me process

Casual drinks at yours is the perfect call — low stakes, home turf, easy escape routes if anyone needs a breather. And a board game is actually smart because it gives everyone something to focus on besides the "so YOU'RE the new person" energy.

Seriously though — everything you're describing sounds really healthy: clean ending with Soph, supportive friends, no pressure with Mika. You're handling it well. Enjoy getting to know them without putting pressure on it. And anytime you need to type-process, go for it. Take care!
